channel.title: AI Firehose: What Is New
channel.description: We read the whole AI firehose so you do not have to, then distill it into one brief: what is new, what is breaking out, and what is worth your time, across the day, week, month, and quarter. You take what you want and leave the rest.

item.title: AI Resume Builder — How to Automate Job Applications & Resume Writing
item.description: AI Master: Jobright is an AI Resume Builder platform with 2M+ users that automates job search workflows including resume tailoring, application tracking, role matching, and cover letter generation. The tool includes a Chrome extension for autofilling applications and an AI career coach feature.

item.title: Sakana Fugu (Fully Tested - V/S Fable): UHM... REALLY?
item.description: AICodeKing: Sakana Fugu is a learned router and multi-agent orchestration system available in two versions. It benchmarks competitively on Terminal Bench, LiveCodeBench, and GPQA Diamond but underperforms Fable 5 on SWE Bench Pro and shows mixed results in practical coding and visual generation tasks.

item.title: NEW Claude Design Update Changes EVERYTHING!
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Julian Goldie SEO discusses a design update to Claude, claiming it changes everything. The video promotes AI tools and courses for SEO and business automation.

item.title: This NEW Hermes Update Builds Agents From Scratch
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Hermes introduces a Blank Slate mode that lets users build AI agents from scratch by starting with minimal components and adding only necessary tools. This approach reduces bloat, prevents unwanted feature drift from updates, and simplifies agent configuration for lean use cases.

item.title: Closer to the Material, Ryo Lu | Compile 26
item.description: Cursor: Ryo Lu discusses how AI changes software development workflows and what aspects of craft must be retained. Topics include the black box risk of AI abstractions, the importance of staying close to material reality rather than abstracting away from it, and the role of tools like Cursor in preserving connection to underlying systems.

item.title: This NEW NotebookLM Update Changes Everything
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Google's NotebookLM has been upgraded to function as a full AI agent capable of writing code, building sources, and delivering finished outputs autonomously. New features include a cloud computer for handling code and documents, auto-source building from initial ideas, and a Studio for generating podcasts and reports with cited answers.

item.title: Gemma Playground: Parallel Agents in Action
item.description: Google for Developers: Ian Ballantyne from Google DeepMind demonstrates Gemma 4's multi-agent capabilities, running 10 independent subagents locally with an orchestrator pattern. The system achieves inference speeds above 170 tokens per second and autonomously generates an SVG art gallery through parallel task delegation.

item.title: Claude Code AI SEO: How to Rank #1 with Claude Code
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Julian Goldie presents an agentic SEO system that integrates Claude Code with a user's own Google Search Console data to identify rankable keywords and automatically generate and deploy SEO content across multiple sites. The approach bypasses common keyword tools to find less competitive opportunities, with a demonstrated case of increasing site traffic from 2 to 200+ daily clicks.

item.title: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence with Brian Yu - Chapter 7 - Moving (live, unedited)
item.description: CS50: CS50's Introduction to Artificial Intelligence with Brian Yu is Chapter 7 on Moving, part of Harvard University's foundational computer science curriculum. The course is available through multiple platforms including edX, Harvard Extension School, and OpenCourseWare, with an active community across Discord, GitHub, and social media.

item.title: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence with Brian Yu - Chapter 6 - Generating (live, unedited)
item.description: CS50: CS50 is Harvard University's introduction to computer science course. Chapter 6 focuses on generative AI topics. The course is available through multiple platforms including edX, Harvard Extension School, and OpenCourseWare.

item.title: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence with Brian Yu - Chapter 5 - Communicating (live, unedited)
item.description: CS50: CS50 is Harvard University's introductory computer science course that includes a chapter on artificial intelligence and communicating with AI systems. The course is available through multiple platforms including edX, Harvard Extension School, and OpenCourseWare, with this specific session taught by Brian Yu focusing on communication topics.

item.title: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence with Brian Yu - Chapter 4 - Sensing (live, unedited)
item.description: CS50: CS50 is Harvard University's introduction to computer science course. This video is Chapter 4 on Sensing from the "Introduction to Artificial Intelligence with Brian Yu" module, presented as a live unedited lecture.

item.title: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence with Brian Yu - Chapter 3 - Analyzing (live, unedited)
item.description: CS50: CS50 is Harvard University's introductory computer science course taught by David J. Malan. Chapter 3 focuses on analyzing artificial intelligence topics. The course is available through multiple platforms including edX, Harvard Extension School, Harvard Summer School, and OpenCourseWare with supporting communities across various social media and collaboration platforms.

item.title: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence with Brian Yu - Chapter 2 - Predicting (live, unedited)
item.description: CS50: CS50's Introduction to Artificial Intelligence with Brian Yu is Harvard University's foundational course on AI, covering topics including prediction in Chapter 2. The course is available through edX, Harvard Extension School, Harvard Summer School, and OpenCourseWare, with an active community across multiple social platforms.

item.title: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence with Brian Yu - Chapter 1 - Playing (live, unedited)
item.description: CS50: CS50 is Harvard University's introduction to computer science and programming, taught by David J. Malan. The course is available through multiple platforms including edX, Harvard Extension School, Harvard Summer School, and OpenCourseWare. This particular video is Chapter 1 on Playing from an Introduction to Artificial Intelligence track.

item.title: Opening Keynote, Michael Truell | Compile 26
item.description: Cursor: Cursor announced Cursor Mobile, Origin (an agent-native Git platform), and updates to cloud agents at its inaugural Compile conference. The company also revealed a new model trained from scratch as part of its shift toward agent-first architecture.

item.title: NEW Hermes Agent Updates Change Everything!
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Hermes 0.17 introduces a framework for building teams of autonomous AI agents with async sub-agents, loop engineering for quality control, and an Agent Operating System. The update enables no-code automation and business content automation at scale.

item.title: NEW AI SEO Agent is INSANE!
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Julian Goldie promotes a new AI SEO agent designed for automating search engine optimization tasks. The agent is positioned as a powerful tool integrated with Agent OS and promoted through his AI Profit Lab community.

item.title: Rethinking the ROI of cybersecurity
item.description: IBM Technology: IBM Technology argues that security spending should not impede innovation. The video advocates for strategic risk thinking that balances security protection with business growth and speed to market.

item.title: Are AI Glasses Over? + Big Technology AI Summit Audience Questions
item.description: Alex Kantrowitz: Alex Kantrowitz and Ranjan Roy discuss whether AR glasses represent the future of AI devices, exploring ideal form factors, device utility, and broader questions about AI model applications and corporate responsibility. The discussion covers Snapchat Specs, the pace of technological change, US-China AI relations, and biology applications.

item.title: Gemini 3.5 Pro LEAKS is INSANE!
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Julian Goldie SEO discusses leaked features of Gemini 3.5 Pro, characterizing them as remarkable. The video promotes his AI-related courses and services including Agent OS and AI SEO strategies.

item.title: Get Fable 5 output on a cheaper model
item.description: Jack Roberts: A leaked Fable 5 system prompt can be injected into Claude Opus 4.8 to achieve near-identical output quality at a fraction of the cost. The approach requires only one line of code and is available on GitHub.

item.title: Google’s new AI agent stack from I/O 2026
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google I/O 2026 announced a four-layer AI agent stack including the Gemini 3.5 Flash model, Agent Development Kit 2.0, Agent2Agent Protocol, and Managed Agents API. The stack features graph-based orchestration, enterprise skill registries, and integrates the multimodal Gemini Omni Flash video model for production-ready agent deployment.

item.title: Bring Gemini’s intelligence to AlloyDB using AI functions
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: AlloyDB AI introduces SQL-native functions like ai.generate, ai.rank, ai.summarize, and ai.forecast that bring Gemini intelligence directly into PostgreSQL workflows. The functions provide performance optimizations and cost reductions compared to row-at-a-time LLM calls for tasks like ranking, sentiment analysis, and forecasting.

item.title: AI Location Control: Give Clients FULL Power! #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: A short-form video argues that clients should have full control over AI infrastructure operations and geographical deployment locations. The content emphasizes the importance of transparency and client power in dictating where and how artificial intelligence systems operate.

item.title: Sakana Fugu vs Fusion (Beats Fable 5?)
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Julian Goldie compares Sakana's Fugu (Ultra and Mini) with OpenRouter Fusion via side-by-side tests on visual and code generation tasks. Fugu Ultra ranks first for quality, Fusion second, and Fugu Mini third, though Fugu offers cheaper flat pricing with daily caps while Fusion charges per token with easier API access.

item.title: How Gong Built a $7B+ Company in the Age of Vibe Coding | Amit Bendov
item.description: EO: Amit Bendov, CEO of Gong, argues that software moats depend less on code itself and more on customer context, proprietary data, product experience, and operational complexity. He contends that AI will not eliminate SaaS companies as feared, and emphasizes that the best AI products create devoted users through customer obsession and design excellence.

item.title: Learn 97% of Claude in Under 12 Minutes
item.description: Jack Roberts: Jack Roberts walks through seven levels of Claude functionality, from basic chat to autonomous agents and code generation. Coverage includes model selection between Opus and Sonnet, prompt engineering, integrations with tools like Firecrawl and Gmail, sub-agents for parallel task execution, codebase analysis with Graphify, and the Claude Code operating system for spend tracking.

item.title: What caused this React app to crash?
item.description: Google for Developers: Google for Developers presents a CI pipeline puzzle where a React app crashes in production after upgrading from React 18 to React 19, caused by an unexpected build speedup tied to cache configuration. The video invites viewers to identify the root cause.

item.title: Anthropic's AI Future: GPUs, Power & Unseen Security! #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: A YouTube short discussing Anthropic's AI infrastructure, focusing on GPU costs, power requirements, and security mechanisms like sandboxes and MCP tunnels. The video explores the operational infrastructure needed to support modern AI systems.

item.title: NEW Hermes Agent v0.17.0 is INSANE
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Hermes Agent v0.17.0, called "The Reach Release," is an open-source agent framework that integrates across applications with a new Blank Slate setup mode that lets users start with minimal configuration and add only needed features. The release includes iMessage integration, background sub-agents, persistent memory, and desktop upgrades.

item.title: What it takes to build *realtime* voice models | Voxtral
item.description: Julia Turc: Julia Turc examines Mistral's Voxtral Realtime and Voxtral TTS as examples of modern realtime voice model architecture. The video covers modular vs end-to-end speech design, audio tokenization techniques including vector quantization and finite scalar quantization, and key historical milestones like Whisper, Whisper Streaming, and WaveNet.

item.title: AI Agents: DEAD or ALIVE? Businesses Are Still Waiting! #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: Joao argues that advanced AI agents with sophisticated capabilities already exist today. However, businesses remain hesitant and slow to adopt these tools in practice, creating a gap between technological availability and enterprise deployment.

item.title: AI in the SDLC: Rethinking AI Coding Tools & AI Agents
item.description: IBM Technology: Cedric Clyburn argues that while AI promises speed in software development, productivity gains are limited despite faster coding. He proposes redesigning SDLC workflows with AI agents to improve testing, outcomes, and delivery.

item.title: Stop Prompting. Start Looping (The New AI Way)
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Loop engineering replaces manual prompt iteration by defining success criteria, letting an agent act, and using a judge model to verify results and repeat until the goal is met. The approach includes several implementations: Fusion loops with configurable rounds, agent Kanban boards with planner/builder/reviewer roles, Fusion boardroom combining multiple outputs, and Sakana Fugu council approaches.

item.title: GLM-5.2 + OpenDesign: SOTA CHEAP DESIGN SYSTEM! This is AWESOME!
item.description: AICodeKing: A video demonstration showing how to use GLM-5.2 with OpenDesign to generate high-quality UI prototypes including landing pages, dashboards, and mobile app interfaces. The workflow produces polished HTML and CSS artifacts with consistent design properties. OpenDesign can be installed via desktop app, from source, or Docker.

item.title: Apple Just Built WSL for the Mac (Container Machines)
item.description: Better Stack: Apple Container Machines provides lightweight, persistent Linux environments on Mac, built on Apple's Container project and optimized for Apple Silicon. It offers capabilities like multiple Linux distros, systemd support, home directory sharing, and benchmarks favorably against Docker Desktop and OrbStack.

item.title: AI Adoption Gap: Businesses Are SLOWLY Catching Up! #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: Advanced AI capabilities are developing faster than businesses can adopt them, with agentic AI seeing particularly slow enterprise uptake. This adoption gap represents missed opportunities for companies seeking transformative growth.

item.title: New Agent OS is INSANE!
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Agent OS is an operating system for coordinating multiple AI agents like Hermes and Jarvis in unified workflows. It uses Kanban boards for task triage, supports agent orchestration through group chat and pipelines, and integrates tools like Obsidian and NotebookLM for memory and content generation, with deployment options for SEO automation and other tasks.

item.title: AI Agents: Stop Button Building, Start Startup Speed! #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: Cyril Imhof argues that building AI agents requires agility and startup-style iteration rather than traditional software engineering approaches. He contends that startups like Anthropic adapt faster to the unpredictable nature of AI agents than large technology companies.

item.title: Microsoft & Nvidia Laptop: Local AI Revolutionized! #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: Microsoft integrates Nvidia's chip technology into laptops to enable on-device AI acceleration. This represents a hardware-level approach to local AI processing power.

item.title: LIVE: Sakana VS Claude Fable 5 VS Fusion!
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Julian Goldie tests and compares the Sakana model against Claude Fable 5 and Fusion in a live stream. The session focuses on practical AI applications built with an Agent OS framework for SEO and business automation.

item.title: Sakana: Fable 5 Intelligence WITHOUT Fable 5!
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Sakana released Fugu, a multi-agent orchestration system with a standard and higher-end "Fugu Ultra" variant that uses panel-style synthesis of multiple model responses. The system is accessible via a single model API and reportedly matches frontier models like Claude and others on benchmarks.

item.title: LIVE: NEW Mythos 5.1?
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Julian Goldie SEO promotes an Agent OS product through a live streaming session. The content focuses on building agentic operating systems and monetizing AI tools for time and cost savings.

item.title: I Tested GLM 5.2 vs Opus 4.8 vs GPT 5.5
item.description: Chase AI: Chase AI conducted a video comparison testing GLM 5.2 against Claude Opus 4.8 and GPT 5.5 across DeepSWE Bench and custom test cases to evaluate whether the hyped open-source GLM 5.2 model performs competitively against frontier proprietary models.

item.title: NEW Claude Sonnet 5 coming?
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Julian Goldie SEO creates content discussing rumors or speculation about a potential Claude Sonnet 5 release. The video is paired with promotions for his AI business courses and SEO tools.

item.title: China's New AI Robot MOYA Feels Too Real (92% Human)
item.description: AI Revolution: China's MOYA humanoid robot features lifelike skin and reactions. Boston Dynamics is advancing Atlas toward factory autonomy, while Alibaba launches Qwen-Robot as an embodied AI platform for physical machines. The article highlights rapid progress in humanoid robot capabilities moving beyond demonstrations into real-world deployment.

item.title: Fable 5 Just Got Banned, Here’s What To Do
item.description: Jack Roberts: A video argues that model bans don't eliminate capability, they redirect it elsewhere. The creator suggests frontier model restrictions are temporary ecosystem friction and that alternatives like Opus 4.8 remain available and competitive.

item.title: Checkbook Diplomacy - Sarah Paine
item.description: Dwarkesh Patel: Dwarkesh Patel hosts a conversation with Sarah Paine on the topic of checkbook diplomacy. The discussion covers geopolitical strategy and how nations use financial leverage in diplomatic relations.

item.title: Obsidian Just 10x’d Everyone’s Hermes Agent
item.description: David Ondrej: David Ondrej presents a tutorial on connecting Hermes Agent to Obsidian, claiming a 10x improvement. The video covers AI agent deployment, integration practices, and promotes hosting and educational resources for AI development.
